title: Prefetch, Goroutine Concurrency, and Capacity · Execution Trace: Fair Tenant Dispatch
summary: A Go 1.26 service team must relate Qos prefetch, handler goroutines, bytes, downstream pools, service time, and recovery backlog; reviewers reject hidden goroutine ownership, unbounded work, stale channel state, and claims that stop at a nil client error. The learner predicts values, ownership transitions, and stop paths before seeing a model. Planned artifact: Add and verify the fair tenant dispatch increment in a measured consumer-capacity budget. Learners progress through prediction, explici…
objectives: Prevent one tenant or expensive message class from monopolizing consumers using topology, admission, bounded scheduling, and separate evidence; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.
Change prefetch only with bounded measurement windows, hysteresis, safe limits, in-flight accounting, and rollback evidence; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and Go Predict Fair Tenant Dispatch · Theory Fair Tenant Dispatch
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory Go messaging team handles case 8415 during canary Go consumer decoding old and new envelopes. Build deterministic Go evidence to combine prefetch, active goroutines, dependency capacity, and message-byte budgets; keep credentials and connection URLs out of output; then lower the byte and goroutine budget and preserve rejection evidence.
